mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL > 250, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 months of regime onset.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 255 while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, markets price in delayed monetary tightening and stagflation risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined signal of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS does not precede negative 5-day returns, or the interaction effect reverses direction in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0% signals severe demand contraction, prompting equity market repricing for lower earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%, or the threshold signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad supply-side contraction and labor market weakness, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
